'''Schinga shikou''' were large, predatory, serpents native to [[Makem Te/Legends|Makem Te]], and the most prized of the [[schinga]] species. A pureblood schinga shikou was roughly eight meters long, with smooth scales along the length of its body and dozens of large white fangs. It held its head upright as it slithered. 

Some were domesticated by the native [[Swokes Swokes/Legends|Swokes Swokes]], for whom schinga shikou breeding was a profitable business. Due to careful breeding, there were dozens of varieties of schinga. [[Color/Legends|Red]], gold, and white specimens were especially prized. The Schinga shikou had several relatives, such as the venomous [[Schinga ikkow]], the subterranean-dwelling [[Schinga klaug]], the small [[Schinga picai]], and the strong [[Schinga equa]].
